_meta.notes: The homepage is bespoke. Typical sections from the brief: hero video / hero image with overlay text, services strip (architecture / civil / structural / interior / historic), 'WE BELIEVE IN' repeating block list, a featured projects strip, a stats counter strip, contact/CTA at the bottom. This schema captures the variable repeater of believe-in blocks plus other named sections we surface during extraction.

prompt: Extract the warehausae.com homepage's structured content: hero overlay text, services strip with discipline labels + URLs, the 'WE BELIEVE IN' repeater blocks, a featured projects list, any stats counters, testimonials, and the CTA copy.
systemPrompt: Extracting the homepage. Use null or empty arrays for missing fields.

hero_overlay_heading.description: Primary heading shown over the hero (often 'We listen. We design. We deliver.' or similar).

hero_image_url.description: URL of the hero background image (or first frame of the hero video).

hero_video_url.description: URL of the hero video if used.

services_strip.description: Service link tiles below the hero (architecture, civil, structural, interior, historic).

believe_in_blocks.description: Repeating 'WE BELIEVE IN' blocks. Order matters.

featured_projects.description: Featured project tiles on the homepage.

label.description: What the counter measures (e.g. 'Years of Experience', 'Projects Delivered').

value_text.description: The displayed value, often with suffix (e.g. '45+', '500').

cta_heading.description: Heading of the final contact / CTA section (e.g. 'Have a project in mind?').

cta_subheading.description: Subheading of the CTA section (e.g. 'Let's talk.').

cta_body.description: Optional supporting prose for the CTA.
